title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR FERMENTED SOYBEAN PASTE Asia CXS 298R-2009

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR FERMENTED SOYBEAN PASTE Asia CXS 298R-2009
sections.summary: Document title: Regional Standard for Fermented Soybean Paste, Asia (CXS 298R-2009).
Administrative note stating the standard was adopted in 2009 and amended in 2012, 2013, 2020 and 2023.
Notes that, following Codex Commission decisions in December 2023, amendments were made to Section 8.3 on labelling of non-retail containers.
Scope: applies to the product defined in Section 2 when offered for direct consumption (including catering or repacking) and excludes product indicated as intended for further processing.

Defines fermented soybean paste as a paste-type fermented food whose essential ingredient is soybean and specifies manufacturing steps: use of boiled/steamed soybeans (alone or with grains) fermented with microorganisms, mixing with salt/brine, aging until quality meets Section 3.2, and heat or other processing before or after hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage.

Defines the product as a soybean-based fermented paste and requires manufacturing via fermentation of boiled/steamed soybean (or soybean/grain mix), mixing with salt/brine, aging until Section 3.2 quality is achieved, and heat or other processing and hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage.

Lists basic ingredients required for fermented soybean paste: soybeans, salt, potable water, and naturally occurring or cultivated microorganisms (Bacillus spp. and/or Aspergillus spp.) which must not be pathogenic and must not produce toxins.

Permits optional ingredients including grains/flours (wheat, rice, barley, etc.), yeast/yeast extracts, Lactobacillus/Lactococcus, distilled ethyl alcohol from specified agricultural sources, sugars, starch syrup, and natural flavouring raw materials (dried fish/seaweed powders or extracts, spices, herbs, etc.).

Specifies composition and quality factors: minimum total nitrogen and amino nitrogen and maximum moisture depending on whether product is made from soybean only or soybean-plus-grains (total nitrogen ≥1.6% or ≥0.6%; amino nitrogen ≥0.3% or ≥0.12%; moisture ≤60%), requires characteristic flavour/odour/colour/texture and notes a nitrogen conversion factor of 5.71; it also states any container failing Section 3.2 is defective and a lot is acceptable when defectives do not exceed the sampling-plan acceptance number (c).

Sets numeric quality limits: for soybean-only paste total nitrogen ≥1.6% and amino nitrogen ≥0.3%; for soybean-plus-grains total nitrogen ≥0.6% and amino nitrogen ≥0.12%; moisture ≤60% for both, product must have characteristic flavour/odour/colour/texture, and the nitrogen conversion factor 5.71 should be used.
States that any container failing to meet the quality requirements of Section 3.2 shall be considered a defective.

Provides lot-acceptance criterion: a lot meets the quality requirements of Section 3.2 when the number of defectives (per Section 3.3) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plans.

Permits use of acidity regulators, antioxidants, colours, flavour enhancers, preservatives, stabilizers and sweeteners listed in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) and specifies maximum levels for certain additives by INS number (see subsections), while also listing allowable processing aids such as protease, hemicellulase, lipase, lactic acid and selected polyphosphates and esters.

Permits specific acidity regulators: L(+)-tartaric acid (INS 334) up to 1000 mg/kg and allows sodium L(+)-tartrate (INS 335(ii)) and potassium sodium L(+)-tartrate (INS 337) as tartaric acid equivalents.
Permits the antioxidant sodium thiosulphate (INS 539) at a maximum of 30 mg/kg expressed as sulphur dioxide.
Permits synthetic riboflavin (INS 101(i)) as a colour additive at a maximum of 10 mg/kg.

Permits preservatives including sorbic acid (INS 200) and benzoic acid (INS 210) each up to 1000 mg/kg, and potassium/calcium sorbates and sodium/potassium benzoates (INS 202/203/211/212) singly or in combination as indicated.
Permits sweeteners acesulfame potassium (INS 950) up to 350 mg/kg and sodium saccharin (INS 954(iv)) up to 200 mg/kg.

Lists permitted processing aids for the product, giving INS numbers and names (Protease; Hemicellulase; Lipase; 472c Citric and fatty acid esters of glycerol; 270 Lactic acid; 452(i) Sodium polyphosphates, glassy; 452(ii) Potassium polyphosphates).

Requires products to comply with the maximum levels in the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends products be prepared and handled per the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex hygiene texts, and to comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Sets a minimum fill requirement (product must occupy not less than 90% of the container's water capacity, allowing necessary headspace), declares containers failing that requirement as defective, and states lot acceptance is based on the number of defectives not exceeding the sampling plan acceptance number (c), with some product types exempt from minimum fill.

Requires containers to be filled so the product occupies at least 90% of the container's water capacity (minus any necessary headspace per GMP), defines water capacity as the volume of distilled water at 20 °C the sealed container holds, and notes minimum fill may not apply to some product types.
States that any container failing to meet the Section 7.1 minimum fill requirement shall be classified as defective.
Provides lot acceptance criterion: a lot meets the minimum fill requirement when the number of defectives (per Section 7.2) does not exceed the allowable number (c) in the applicable sampling plan.
Requires products to be labelled in accordance with the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).

Specifies the product name shall be "Fermented soybean paste", while allowing other names if permitted by national legislation and permitting inclusion of an ingredient name that characterizes the product.
Heading only; this section contains no substantive text or requirements regarding 'Halal' claims.
States that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Heading only; no substantive provisions are given in this section on methods of analysis and sampling (details appear in subsequent subsections).
Mandates determination of total nitrogen according to AOAC method 984.13.

Specifies determination of amino nitrogen by AOAC 920.154 B (Sorensen Method) with sample preparation (2 g sample extracted with 100 ml cold NH3‑free water for 60 min, filtered to 100 ml) and requires using a pH meter to determine the endpoint instead of optical colour verification.
Specifies moisture determination according to AOAC 934.01 using a drying temperature of 70 °C or lower.
Contains bibliographic notes and references to Codex standards and guidelines cited in the document; not substantive requirements.
